patches.summary: Route generated read endpoints through Jobber GraphQL queries with OAuth refresh handling.
patches.reason: Jobber is GraphQL-only, while the generated CLI emitted REST-shaped paths; the CLI must remain read-only.
patches.validated_outcome: go build ./... and go vet ./... pass with GOCACHE set to a writable temp directory.

patches.summary: Promote `clients` and `jobs` parents to top level; rename Cobra Use `jobber-jobs` to `jobs`; remove dropped-novel sections from README.
patches.reason: Transport rewrite is done; the generated parents were Hidden:true (press v4.9.0 default for multi-endpoint parents) and `jobber-jobs` carried a press-collision-era prefix that no longer applies. README also still referenced novels (jobs pnl / payouts reconcile / jobs stale / funnel / clients 360) that were dropped from locked scope.
patches.validated_outcome: Top-level --help now lists `clients` and `jobs` alongside other resources; `go build ./...` and `go vet ./...` pass.

patches.summary: Teach the scaffolder-generated sync envelope walker to recognize Relay's pageInfo wrapper and hasNextPage flag.
patches.reason: GraphQL transport returns canonical Relay shape {nodes:[...], pageInfo:{hasNextPage, endCursor}}. sync.go's extractPaginationFromEnvelope already recurses into 4 wrapper objects but pageInfo was not one of them, and the hasMore key list did not include hasNextPage. Without this, sync terminates after page 1 against Jobber.
patches.validated_outcome: go build ./... and go vet ./... pass; envelope walker now extracts endCursor from pageInfo.endCursor and hasMore from pageInfo.hasNextPage.

patches.summary: Add local read-only AR aging, invoice trace, and snapshot save/diff commands.
patches.reason: These novel commands answer offline analysis workflows that are not generated from Jobber's GraphQL API surface and must survive regeneration as local customizations.
patches.validated_outcome: go build ./..., go vet ./..., staged binary rebuild, and help checks for root/ar aging/invoices trace/snapshot all pass; invoices list resolves.

patches.summary: Add read-only ad-hoc SQL command (sql) against the local synced DB with defense-in-depth write-token rejection and cell truncation.
patches.reason: A19 sql is part of the locked 16-command surface. The press scaffolder does not emit it because it's not derivable from the API spec. Local novel command that must survive regeneration.
patches.validated_outcome: go build ./..., go vet ./..., staged binary rebuild, sql help/root help, sql table smoke, sql --explain smoke, and DROP rejection pass with a workspace smoke DB; default DB smoke reports sync hint because the local synced DB is absent.

patches.summary: Address all four Greptile findings from PR #816: serialize OAuth refresh under a sync.Mutex with stale-token double-check, tighten cache file permissions to 0o600/0o700, gate the Windows-only PowerShell token-persist on runtime.GOOS, and add WITH to the read-only SQL validator's banned-token regex.
patches.reason: PR #816 surfaced one P0 (concurrent RefreshAccessToken can race the rotation chain — directly violates the standing refresh-token persistence constraint), two P1 hardening items (cache files were 0o644 world-readable; PowerShell exec ran unconditionally on non-Windows hosts and produced noisy stderr), and a defense-in-depth item on the SQL allowlist (CTE-wrapped writes like `WITH x AS (...) DELETE FROM t` were caught by SQLite read-only mode but bypassed the validator regex). All four must land before resubmission so the Greptile thread is fully resolved.
patches.validated_outcome: go build ./... clean; refreshIfStale double-checks Config.AuthHeader() under the lock so a second concurrent 401 reuses the rotated token instead of replaying the invalidated one; cache writes now use 0o700/0o600; PowerShell persist is no-op outside windows; SQL validator rejects WITH in addition to the prior DML/DDL/PRAGMA set.

patches.summary: Address all four Greptile findings from PR #833 re-review: propagate the cache-permission tightening to the standalone internal/cache package, switch ar aging to OpenReadOnly, gate AR Total/InvoiceCount on successful bucket assignment, and tighten snapshot directory permissions to 0o700.
patches.reason: Greptile confidence score on PR #833 was 3/5 with four follow-on findings. (1) The PR #816 cache fix only landed in client.go's writeCache; the standalone internal/cache/cache.go package still wrote 0o755/0o644 with raw API responses on disk. (2) ar aging opened the SQLite DB read-write via OpenWithContext while every other read path uses OpenReadOnly. (3) addARAgingBucket silently dropped balances from the four aging buckets when due_date was missing/unparseable, but the caller unconditionally added the balance to row.Total and incremented InvoiceCount — so bucket sums no longer matched Total. (4) snapshot save created its snapshots directory with 0o755 even though the snapshot files themselves were 0o600.
patches.validated_outcome: go build ./... clean; internal/cache writes use 0o700/0o600 matching client.go; ar aging opens the DB read-only; AR Total and InvoiceCount only advance when addARAgingBucket succeeds (returns true), so bucket sums equal the per-client total; snapshots directory is 0o700.
